title: QUANTUM BUNDLES AND QUANTUM INTERACTIONS
journal: International Journal of Geometric Methods in Modern Physics
publisher: World Scientific Pub Co Pte Lt
abstract: A geometric framework for describing quantum particles on a possibly curved background is proposed. Natural constructions on certain distributional bundles ("quantum bundles") over the space–time manifold yield a quantum "formalism" along any 1-dimensional timelike submanifold (a "detector"); in the flat, inertial case this reproduces the basic results of the usual quantum field theory, while in general it could be seen as a local, "linearized" description of the actual physics.
